revision.text:
[[ja:忌部神社 (吉野川市)]] {{Expand Japanese|忌部神社 (吉野川市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inbe Shrine | native_name = 忌部神社 | image = Inbe Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inbe Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.05452777777778|134.26672222222223}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|lt=Amenohiwashi|ja|天日鷲神|ko|아메노히와시노카미|zh|天日鷲神|qid=Q10939697|1=Otori-sama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Inbe Shrine'''|忌部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11657514|1=List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)}} ** Series ordinal: 18 ** Series ordinal: 19 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041319|ltq=Q135041319|qid=Q135041319|1=Yamatoookuni- Shrine}} ** P155: {{ill|Imuheno Shrine|lt=Imuheno Shrine|qid=Q135041321|1=Imuheno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041330|ltq=Q135041330|qid=Q135041330|1=Amamurakumono Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ikagashi Shrine|lt=Ikagashi Shrine|ja|伊加加志神社|qid=Q11378911|1=Ikagashi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q592500|ltq=Q592500|qid=Q592500|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}} * {{ill|Q907457|ltq=Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} * {{ill|Oe District, Tokushima|lt=Oe district|en|Oe District, Tokushima|fa|بخش اوئه، توکوشیما|ja|麻植郡|zh|麻植郡|qid=Q3205811|1=Oe District, Tokushima}} == coordinate location (P625) == * source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060723061802/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/540301.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183192 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Unranked|lt=Unranked|ja|无位|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3480005002908 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Imuheno Shrine|lt=Imuheno Shrine|qid=Q135041321|1=Imuheno Shrine}} * {{ill|Q135041330|ltq=Q135041330|qid=Q135041330|1=Amamurakumono Shrine}} == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Amamurakumo Shrine|lt=Amamurakumo Shrine|qid=Q135198111|1=Amamurakumo Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県吉野川市山川町忌部山14-8', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '徳島県吉野川市山川町村雲', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:忌部神社 (吉野川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11490723}} == Japanese content == __NOTOC__ {{Shrine |Name = Ibe Shrine |Image = [[File:Inbe Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg|280px|Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa City)]]
Oratory |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinogawa City|Q592500|qid=Q592500|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa City)#Region|Q28689703|qid=Q28689703|lt=Yamakawa Town|1=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa)}}{{ill|lt=Yamakawa Town Ibeyama|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibeyama}}14-8 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Tenhi Eagle God|Q10939697|qid=Q10939697|lt=Tenhi Eagle Shoya|1=Otori-sama}} |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) Ronsha
Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}2 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Gabled structure|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 19th|Q2961|qid=Q2961|1=October 19}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Ibe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} ``Inbe Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinogawa City|Q592500|qid=Q592500|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa City)#Region|Q28689703|qid=Q28689703|lt=Ibeyama, Yamakawa Town|1=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa)}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tokushima Prefecture {{ill|lt=Asae District#Shikinai Shrine|Q3205811|qid=Q3205811|lt=Asae District|1=Oe District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Yamakawa Town (Tokushima Prefecture)#History|Q8047832|qid=Q8047832|lt=Yamakawa Town|1=Yamakawa, Tokushima}}. It is {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and the former {{ill|lt=Shakaku|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (temporarily {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917280|lt=Kokuheichusha|1=Kokuhei Chūsha}}). Another name is ``Yamasaki Inbe Shrine.'' === History === In the ``Kogo Shui'' section of the ritual utensils section, {{ill|lt=Amantomi no Mikoto|Q24899653|qid=Q24899653|1=Amatomi no Mikoto}}, the grandchild of Tentadama no Mikoto, is shown as a grain tree and {{ill|lt=Asa|Q79817 In search of suitable land to plant|qid=Q79817 In search of suitable land to plant|lt=hemp}}, the Ibe clan of Awa led the grandsons of Hiwasi no Mikoto to {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and settled there. |Q11433653}} It is written that cotton and {{ill|lt=linen fabric|Q11239515|qid=Q11239515|lt=linen cloth|1=Hemp cloth}} came to be offered as tribute, but after that, the rough cloth woven by Mr. It was customary to offer Aratae Miso (aratae miso). In addition, on the mountainside called "Kuroiwa" behind this shrine, there is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibeyama Kofun Group|1=UNKNOWN}}, which consists of five {{ill|lt=circular tombs|Q11394747|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} that are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. This suddenly appeared around the 6th century, and it has been pointed out that there may have been a clan group that immigrated to the area. Tokushima Prefectural Museum, ed. "Awa no Inabe clan", Tokushima Prefectural Museum, 1977. .According to the shrine's legend, it began when the Inabe clan of Awa enshrined their ancestor Tenhiwashi no Mikoto on February 25th (lunar calendar)|Q839178|February 25th}} in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, and it is said that it was originally enshrined at the above-mentioned ``Kuroiwa''. Since ancient times, it has been known as the {{ill|lt=tutelary deity|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} from the Awaimbe clan, and also {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Datong|1=Daidō}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}), there were 20 houses. ('{{ill|lt=Shinshokakuchofusho|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'), so it seems that he was respected by the imperial court, but the details are unknown. According to the company journal, in the medieval period, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|qid=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|qid=Q19729|1=1185}}), {{ill|lt=Kono Tsushin|Q11554270|qid=Q11554270|1=Kawano Michinobu}} was published by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nagamitsu|1=UNKNOWN}}, and during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Yashima|Q2117322|qid=Q2117322|1=Battle of Yashima}}, {{ill|lt=Yoshitsune Minamoto|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} and {{ill|lt=Yoichi Nasu|Q2625800|qid=Q2625800|1=Nasu no Yoichi}} dedicated swords and bows and arrows to pray for victory. , the following year, in the 3rd year of the Bunji era, {{ill|lt=Nariyoshi Taguchi|Q11576353|qid=Q11576353|1=Nariyoshi Taguchi}} was ordered by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} to {{ill|lt=Itano District|Q1156025|qid=Q1156025|lt=Bando/Bandishi|1=Itano District, Tokushima}} and {{ill|lt=Awa District|Q48 29634|qid=Q48 29634|lt=Awa}} and {{ill|lt=Miyoshi District|Q1156031|qid=Q1156031|lt=Miyoshi|1=Miyoshi District, Tokushima}}, 1,000 towns of rice fields were carved out and donated as offerings to the 4 districts, conveying the reverence of the samurai Included in Asae County Magazine (1922) edited by the Asae County Educational Association. . Also, according to the company journal ``Yamazaki Saibe Shrine Chronicles,'' it collapsed to its current location due to an earthquake in the {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} years (1394-1428), and was enshrined as a small shrine next to the Oji Gongen, which was at its current location. . After that, {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}3 years ({{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|qid=Q6613|1=1560}}) {{ill|lt=Kanrei|Q1202497|qid=Q1202497|1=Kanrei}}{{ill|lt=Hosokawa clan|Q27 36120|qid=Q27 36120|lt=Hosokawa}} When Sanuki no kami ({{ill|lt=Masayuki Hosokawa|Q11605961|qid=Q11605961|lt=Masayuki|1=Hosokawa Saneyuki}}) {{ill|lt=rested|Q11450498|qid=Q11450498|1=ando}}, {{ill|lt=Masayuki|Q11450498|qid=Q11450498|1=ando}} Osamu|Q11354965}} also attacked this, but it was abolished by {{ill|lt=Motochika Chosokabe|Q1070082|qid=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, and it declined due to the devastation caused by the war, and even its whereabouts were unknown during the domain era. (One theory is that the {{ill|lt=Tokushima clan|Q3034557|qid=Q3034557|lt=Lord of the Tokushima domain|1=Tokushima Domain}}{{ill|lt=Hachisuka clan|Q2975261|qid=Q2975261|1=Hachisuka clan}} deliberately did not restore the shrine territory in order to prevent it from being restored.) In addition, in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 12 years ({{ill|lt=1727|Q6942|qid=Q6942|1=1727}}), the Soun Minbu of Taneho Shrine (the summit of Mt. Taneho|Q60997764}} of Mt. Kawada, Yamakawa-cho, Yoshinogawa City) was A Shinto priest expelled our Shinto priest Mura Kumo Shodayu and embezzled the property, and in the {{ill|lt=Original|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} years (1736-41), the same shrine was also enshrined with the god of Inbe.Fearing controversy, Shikibu's son burned down the shrine, but the old Yamazaki Village (currently In response to a petition from the villagers (1 area in the northeastern part of Yamakawa Town), in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 13 years ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|qid=Q6877|1=1801}}), he dismissed the Soun Shikibu and adopted a granddaughter of Katsudayu Murakumo, who succeeded him as a Shinto priest. However, as seen in the section on ``Shinden'', the main shrine is said to have been built in 1995, so there is some confusion as to whether it was destroyed by fire. .{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), he was listed at the village shrine, but as mentioned above, his whereabouts were unknown after the early modern period, so in the following 4th year, Shikinaiibe Shrine, which had been ranked as a Kokuhei Chusha (locality unknown), was promoted to Kokuhei Chusha in the following year in 5 years after the Japanese scholar {{ill|lt=Eson Kosugi|Q6536257|qid=Q6536257|1=Kosugi Sugimura}} determined that it was our shrine. However, {{jalink|Gosho Shrine in {{ill|lt=Mima District|Q1156496|qid=Q1156496|1=Mima District, Tokushima}} Nishibatayama (currently {{ill|lt=Tsurugi Town|Q1206005|qid=Q1206005|1=Tsurugi, Tokushima}} Sadamitsu) claimed Shikinaiibe Shrine. (Tsurugi Town)|Q119929720|Gosho Shrine}} There was a counterargument, and in the same year 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}), the shrine was re-identified. Although 44008|Dajokan Decree}} was issued, intense controversy continued after that, and in 1881, Gosho Shrine was changed to Shikinai Inabe Shrine, and the shrine returned to being a village shrine. By the way, this time Yamazaki side objected to Gosho Shrine Hibiden, so {{ill|lt=Dajokan (Meiji period)|Q125768223|qid=Q125768223|lt=Dajokan|1=Dajōkan}} decided to establish a new shrine in {{ill|lt=Tokushima City|Q207309|qid=Q207309|lt=Tokushima City|1=Tokushima (city)}} as the current {{ill|lt=Ibe Shrine|Q11490722|qid=Q11490722|1=Inbe Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nikenya-cho|Q11371805|qid=Q11371805|1=Nikenya-cho}}.At the Daijosai festival in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}2 ({{ill|lt=1990|Q2064|qid=Q2064|1=1990}}), the {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} |Asa|Q79817|Aratae, which was woven from hemp in the precincts of this shrine, was presented after a ritual was performed by the chief priest of Ibe Shrine in Tokushima City.{{Cite book|Japanese book|author=|title=Weekly Japanese Shrines No. 67 Inabe Shrine Cannahiko Shrine |publisher=DeAgostini Japan|date=2015-5-28|page=}}. Also, at the Daijo Festival in the first year of {{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|qid=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} ({{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|qid=Q25274|1=2019}}), hemp grown in {{ill|lt=Mima City|Q857828|qid=Q857828|1=Mima, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Kiyadaira|Q28689802|qid=Q28689802|1=Koyadaira}} was woven into manfuku on the grounds and presented.{{Cite web|Japanese book|date=2019-10-28 |url=https://www.topics.or.jp/articles/-/276145 |title=Aratae completed, departure ceremony at two locations in Tokushima Prefecture for Daijo Festival |website={{ill|lt=Tokushima Shimbun|Q11489835|qid=Q11489835|1=Tokushima Shimbun}}Electronic version |publisher=Tokushima Shimbunsha |accessdate=2021-02-02}}. === Company name === Before {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was called "Tenhiwashi Shrine". Furthermore, as will be explained later, since it was enshrined within the precincts of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oji Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}, in early modern times it was simply written as ``''Oji Gongen''''. === God level === In {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=849|Q23902|qid=Q23902|1=849}}), he was conferred the rank of Junior Fifth Rank ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}"), {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan|1=Jōgan}} in the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), he rose to the rank of Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank) along with Marijuanahikojin, and in the second year of {{ill|lt=Genkei|Q747451|qid=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|lt=878|Q23910|qid=Q23910|1=878}}) He was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi in 1996, and to the lower rank of junior fourth rank in 1997 (see above in ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') As it is said that he was promoted from the upper rank of Shogoi in the 7th year of Genkei, it seems that he was promoted to the upper rank of Shogoi between 2000 and 1970, but no records are kept. , in the Engi system, it is listed as {{ill|lt=Shakaku|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|lt=Kanpei Taisha|1=Shakaku}} and entrusted to both {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|lt=Monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niname|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and is also listed as Meishin Taisha. === Shrine building === The main shrine has a {{ill|lt=gabled structure|Q13308575|qid=Q13308575|1=Kirizuma-style}} flat shingled roof, and {{ill|lt=chigi/katsuoki|Q87220234|qid=Q87220234|1=chigi and katsuogi}} is placed on the ridge. According to the ridge tag, it was rebuilt in the 7th year of Kyoho ({{ill|lt=1722|Q6918|qid=Q6918|1=1722}}). === Others === In the precincts there is the ``Ruin of the Manfuku Oriden'', which is said to be the remains of the loom where the {{ill|lt=Miki clan (Awa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miki clan}} (Mitsukishi}}, who are descendants of the Awa-Ibe clan, wove rough clothes for the Daijo festival. === Enshrined God === In the note for ``Ibe Shrine'' in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it says ``A certain name: Asushoku-kami. A certain name: Tenhi-washi-kami.'', which indicates that it is ``Mashoku-kami'' (Oen-kami), also known as {{ill|lt=Amani-washi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Amani-washi-kami}}, but the current deities enshrined are as follows. ; {{ill|lt=Tenhiwashi Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenhiwashi Shoya no Mikoto}} : He is the {{ill|lt=ancestor deity|Q85882782|qid=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of Awa {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}} at this shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=main enshrined deity|1=UNKNOWN}}. ; Empress Iirame no Mikoto : Even if it is the second deity of the main enshrined deity, I can't see anything else.
; {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Tenta Gyokuson|1=Futodama}} : He is {{ill|lt=ancestor god|Q85882782|qid=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}. ; Empress {{ill|lt=Tenhiritō Mikoto|Q85879124|qid=Q85879124|lt=Hiri Noume no Mikoto|1=Ame no Hiratome}} (Hirinome no Mikoto) : She is the dowager god of Tenta Tamason (see {{ill|lt=Susaki Shrine|Q11556513|qid=Q11556513|1=Susaki Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Sunomiya Shrine|Q11556507|qid=Q11556507|1=Sunomiya Shrine}}).
; Tsukuimino Mikoto: ``{{ill|lt=Ancient Japanese Collection|Q531555|qid=Q531555|1=Kogo Shūi}}'' {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}'s {{ill|lt=Amaniwato|Q3231835|qid=Q3231835|lt=Amaniwa Cave|1=Amano-Iwato}}, along with the heavenly eagle-no-mikoto, planted a {{ill|lt=casinoki|Q389185|qid=Q389185|lt=grain tree|1=Paper mulberry}} and {{ill|lt=cotton (Yuu)|Q11519320|qid=Q11519320|1=cotton (Yuu)}}, but there is also a legend that it is another name for ``Kannabihikojin'', the child deity of the sun and eagle life, and the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Kannabihiko Shrine|Q723417|qid=Q723417|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}}{{ill|lt=Shimodate Matsubara Shrine|Q11361262|qid=Q11361262|1=Shimotate Matsubara Shrine}} (Takiguchi, Shirahama-cho, Minamiboso City, Chiba Prefecture), the Inabe clan genealogy is handed down in the former {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} of {{ill|lt=Awa Shrine|Q705547|qid=Q705547|1=Awa Shrine}} (Tateyama City, Chiba Prefecture). ; {{ill|lt=Nagashiraha no Mikoto|Q11653519|qid=Q11653519|lt=Nagashiraha no Mikoto|1=Nagashirahano no Kami}} : Similarly, it can be seen on the steps of Amaterasu Omikami's ghostly residence in the Tengan Cave in the Kogo Shui, and is said to be the ancestral deity of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masatsugu clan|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}.
; Yufushu no Mikoto : The ancestral god of Awa {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, said to be the grandson of Tenhiwashi-no-Mikoto and the child of Cannabihikokami The genealogy listed above. ; Iorihime no Mikoto : I can't see anything else. === Sessha === The following seven companies that are located in the former {{ill|lt=Yamazaki Village|Q11468104|qid=Q11468104|1=Yamazaki Village}} village area are considered {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kyotogaisetsusha|1=UNKNOWN}}. ; Mt. Ibe * Hakusan Hime Shrine - Enshrined on Mt. Inbe, Yamakawa Town, and enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Isanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * Kunushi Shrine - Enshrined on Mt. Inbe, Yamakawa Town, and enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} ; Iwato * Iwato Shrine - Situated in Iwato, Yamakawa Town, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Amensekimonbetsukami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Amensekimonbetsu no Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} and Tentatama no Mikoto. * Takemi Shrine - Enshrined in the same location as Iwato Shrine, Tensoku Tateson and {{ill|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} are enshrined here. * Amamuragumo Shrine - Enshrined in Iwato, Yamakawa Town, it enshrines Amamuragumo no Mikoto and Ijiha Yohime no Mikoto. There is also a theory that it resembles the Shikinai small shrine ``Amamura Kumojin Ijiha Yahime Shrine''. ; eastern foot * Wakamiya Shrine - Enshrined at the eastern foot of Yamakawa Town, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Shikaneson|1=Omoikane}} and Choshiraha no Mikoto. ; Gion * Odoyama Shrine - Situated in Gion, Yamakawa Town, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Hayasu Sanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushiinada Hime no Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. File:Odoyama Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg|Odoyama Shrine File:Kunushi Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg|Kunushi Shrine File:Iwato Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg|Iwato Shrine and Asarashi PondFile:Ameno Murakumo Shrine, Yoshinogawa.jpg|Ameno Murakumo Shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Kazumi Nishinomiya|Q11627607|qid=Q11627607|1=Kazutami Nishimiya}} Annotated by Kogo Shui, {{ill|lt=Iwanami Bunko|Q11475322|qid=Q11475322|1=Iwanami Bunko}}, 1985 * Shikinaisha Research Association, Shikinaisha Research Report, Volume 23, Nankaido, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Publishing Department, 1987 === External link === * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/540301.html#jyougan Ibe Shrine] (Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection) * [http://www.genbu.net/data/awa2/inbe_title.htm The World of Awa Inbe] (Tokushima Prefectural Museum) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Tokushima Prefecture}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:徳島県の旧村社]] [[Category:Inbe clan]] [[Category:History of Yoshinogawa]] [[Category:Buildings in Yoshinogawa]]
